explanation_blueprint.body: pH is 7.42, so the overall pH is near normal. Do not let the near-normal pH hide the underlying process.

explanation_blueprint.title: Primary Disorder
explanation_blueprint.body: PaCO2 is 29.5 mmHg, which is low and supports a primary respiratory alkalosis.

explanation_blueprint.title: Diagnosis
explanation_blueprint.body: The overall pattern is respiratory alkalosis with concurrent high anion gap metabolic acidosis.

explanation_blueprint.title: Compensation
explanation_blueprint.body: If this were isolated acute respiratory alkalosis, expected HCO3 would be about 21.9 mmol/L (acceptable range 19.9-23.9), but the measured value is 18.6 mmol/L. That mismatch does not fit a single-process compensation pattern.

explanation_blueprint.title: Anion Gap
explanation_blueprint.body: Anion gap is 143 - (104 + 18.6) = 20.4, which is raised. That confirms an additional high anion gap metabolic signal.

explanation_blueprint.title: Additional Metabolic Process
explanation_blueprint.body: There is an additional metabolic process present: High anion gap metabolic acidosis.

cases.learning_objective: Recognise the classic respiratory alkalosis plus high anion gap metabolic acidosis pattern of salicylate toxicity

explanation_blueprint.title: Ph Status
explanation_blueprint.body: pH is 7.44, so the overall pH is near normal. Do not let the near-normal pH hide the underlying process.

explanation_blueprint.title: Primary Disorder
explanation_blueprint.body: PaCO2 is 21.9 mmHg, which is low and supports a primary respiratory alkalosis.

explanation_blueprint.title: Diagnosis
explanation_blueprint.body: This is the classic salicylate pattern: respiratory alkalosis with concurrent high anion gap metabolic acidosis.

explanation_blueprint.title: Compensation
explanation_blueprint.body: If this were isolated acute respiratory alkalosis, expected HCO3 would be about 20.4 mmol/L (acceptable range 18.4-22.4), but the measured value is 14.4 mmol/L. That mismatch does not fit a single-process compensation pattern.

explanation_blueprint.title: Anion Gap
explanation_blueprint.body: Anion gap is 142 - (104 + 14.4) = 23.6, which is raised. That confirms an additional high anion gap metabolic signal.

explanation_blueprint.title: Additional Metabolic Process
explanation_blueprint.body: There is an additional metabolic process present: High anion gap metabolic acidosis.

cases.explanation: There is an additional metabolic process present: High anion gap metabolic acidosis. If this were isolated acute respiratory alkalosis, expected HCO3 would be about 20.4 mmol/L (acceptable range 18.4-22.4), but the measured value is 14.4 mmol/L. That mismatch does not fit a single-process compensation pattern. Anion gap is 142 - (104 + 14.4) = 23.6, which is raised. That confirms an additional high anion gap metabolic signal. This is the classic salicylate pattern: respiratory alkalosis with concurrent high anion gap metabolic acidosis.
